Thus the maximum correction we can make would be half

Navamsa = Demands correction of 26 min 40 sec max.
Similarly for Dwadasamsa-
The time taken for one rotation of the Dwadasamsa
= 360/12 = 30°
If we accept that the birth could take place in the trine
Nakshatras as well, then-
The duration between two births =10°
That is, the difference between any two such points = 40
mm
Thus the maximum correction we can make would be half
of it, i.e. 20 min. Thus

66 Secrets of Birth Time Rectification
Dwadasamsa =Demands correction of 20 min max.
On tabulating all the four, we get -
*Navamsa-Navamsa =Demands correction of2 min 57.5 sec
max.
*Navamsa-Dwadasamsa = Demands correction of 2 min 13
sec max.
*Navamsa = Demands correction of 26 min 40 sec max.
*Dwadasamsa = Demands correction of 20 min max.
Note the huge difference of correction demands by the
first two and the last two methods.
